subject: DevOps
title: Continuous Integration
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Continuous Integration.

hook.text: Five developers worked on separate branches for a month. Merging them together took another week.

buildup.text: Long-lived branches diverge. The longer you wait to merge, the more painful integration becomes.

discovery.text: CI means merging code into the main branch at least once a day. Automated builds verify every merge.

twist.text: CI isn't just a tool — it's a discipline. Developers must keep the main branch green at all times.

climax.text: Broken builds get fixed immediately. The team treats a red build like a fire alarm, not a suggestion.

punchline.text: Merge daily, build automatically, fix fast. That's CI.

quiz.question: How often should developers merge code in continuous integration?
